summary: Implemented and ran the real Codex session knowledge promotion step. Added a reusable session:promote command that reads local Codex JSONL sessions, extracts repeated preferences/decisions/mistakes/rules via deterministic pattern aggregation, and writes public-safe paraphrased accepted memory plus review records without storing raw transcripts, message text, previews, or absolute local session paths. Ran it against 47 non-empty local Codex sessions / 4477 messages and promoted 15 accepted codex-session-knowledge memories plus a Codex Session Knowledge Profile and promotion reports. Rebuilt Wiki/SQLite indexes; wiki_search retrieves the promoted profile from sqlite_shards. Strengthened public-data safety to scan untracked sync candidates as well as tracked files; public-data safety reports degraded but blocker_count=0. App changes were committed and pushed as 5582bfe.
decisions: Promote only paraphrased aggregate knowledge, not raw Codex content.
Use session refs and redacted message hashes as evidence instead of raw text or local paths.
Treat promoted records as internal behavior/preference memory, not external factual source truth.
Keep current user instructions above promoted session memory.
Strengthen public-data safety by scanning untracked sync candidates before they are committed.
next_steps: Tune retrieval budgets so newly promoted session memories help without swamping durable source/project records.
Add behavior golden cases proving the promoted preferences change future answers versus memory-off baseline.
Review and merge or demote any promoted aggregate memory that is too broad.
Collect fresh live Codex hook proof for full pre-response completion.
search_queries: Codex session knowledge direct local verification evidence plan first pre-response fail closed raw transcripts
